item.title: Keir Starmer to unveil defence plan with £5bn for drones
item.description: Long-delayed blueprint is unlikely to end military figures’ calls for more funding

item.title: FirstFT: Supermicro’s Taiwan offices raided in chip smuggling investigation
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: China grounds light aircraft after Beijing crash and foreign investors fear Japan is backsliding on reform

item.title: Foreign investors fear Japan is backsliding on reform
item.description: Pendulum ‘swinging back towards economic nationalism’, warns one private equity executive

item.title: Maersk raises profit guidance as new US tariffs fuel demand
item.description: American companies rush to stockpile goods ahead of fresh round of levies

item.title: Supermicro Taiwan offices raided in chip smuggling probe
item.description: Server maker’s shares fell about 8% after news of the investigation

item.title: BT’s turnaround is in the home stretch
item.description: A streamlined domestic business and the end of a hefty investment programme mean it will soon start to generate cash

item.title: Former Tory MP pleads guilty to cheating over bets on 2024 election date
item.description: Craig Williams used inside information from his role as a parliamentary aide to Rishi Sunak

item.title: Comcast’s Hollywood break-up marks its GE moment
item.description: Owning glamour businesses might be fun for the CEO and board but is not necessarily useful for shareholders

item.title: ‘The risk of a deleveraging event is rising’
item.description: Morgan Stanley’s analysts are worried about quarter-end money tightness

item.title: Atom Bank sale on cusp of collapse
item.description: Shareholders targeted £600mn valuation but process has failed to attract bids at desired price

item.title: AI money is going to swamp the midterms this year
item.description: Alex Bores’ defeat in a New York congressional primary was a taste of things to come

item.title: Del Vecchio’s €10bn buyout bid hit by financing hurdles
item.description: Holding company’s board refuses to act as backstop for deal to purchase siblings’ stakes

item.title: Head to tail: business grapples with 100 per cent fish
item.description: An ancient habit of frugality resonates with the US Midwest culture of thrift

item.title: FirstFT: AI fuels record number of power sector deals
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Venezuela’s government faces backlash over earthquake response; and how top executives stay fit

item.title: Comcast to spin off NBCUniversal and Sky
item.description: Studio and broadcast business to become separate public companies

item.title: China grounds light aircraft after Beijing tower crash
item.description: Flight operators say authorities issued indefinite nationwide airspace controls

item.title: British American Tobacco slashes 9,000 jobs
item.description: Maker of Dunhill cigarettes says cuts and outsourcing will affect about one in five jobs

item.title: Samsung and SK Hynix plan $600bn chipmaking expansion
item.description: Companies to invest alongside South Korean government in factories to meet surging demand for memory chips

item.title: UK’s Bridgepoint buys real estate unit in $1.4bn bet on US property
item.description: Private capital group is looking beyond its traditional speciality in corporate buyouts

item.title: China targets more Japanese companies with export controls
item.description: Further restrictions on sales of ‘dual-use’ items with both civilian and military purposes

item.title: BT and Verizon to create new international business in $625mn deal
item.description: Joint venture will be milestone in UK group’s strategy of refocusing on its domestic operations

item.title: AInflation is real but tiny
item.description: Plus, European stocks ain’t cheap

item.title: Artificial intelligence and Engels’ Pause
item.description: Politics could be a bigger constraint than power and compute

item.title: European producers press Brussels to shut Russian aluminium loophole
item.description: Also in this newsletter: Pharma sector push EU to close life sciences funding gap

item.title: How Citadel became an energy giant
item.description: Plus, Venezuela’s huge debt pile, Japanese IPO slump and Constantin Brâncuşi in Berlin

item.title: Warsh’s Fed communication is adding volatility to rates markets
item.description: The downsides of seeking more fundamental information from markets will be in greater surprises

item.title: How Vinícius Júnior became central to Brazil’s World Cup dream
item.description: Real Madrid winger holds key to Brazil’s hopes of winning first world title since 2002

item.title: Europe risks starting winter with gas stocks at 15-year low
item.description: Storage facilities in the EU are not being refilled fast enough ahead of colder months

item.title: Virgin Media O2 bonds slide as broadband rivals squeeze cash flow
item.description: Telecoms group’s bonds tumble as altnets take market share and investors fret over £2bn Netomnia deal

item.title: Freight shipping costs surge as companies race to beat new Trump tariffs
item.description: Rates reach highest since 2024 Red Sea crisis in anticipation of fresh levies from US
